style: An illustrated storybook plate matching the Grants Pass chapter slideshows (ch35–ch38): hand-drawn black ink linework with soft watercolor washes on aged cream parchment. NOT a photograph, NOT photoreal, NOT 3D, NOT digital painting with smooth gradients — it is a comic/storybook illustration with visible ink outlines and watercolor texture. Outer margin is soft cream parchment with a faint aged-paper texture and a thin decorative rectangular frame; optional small green Art Nouveau corner flourishes. A short handwritten cursive caption in the lower corner (e.g. "Grants Pass, 1903"). COLOR BEHAVIOR: often mostly monochrome sepia/gray/charcoal ink with ONE selective vivid emerald-green accent for a key object or detail; warmer scenes may use soft amber, wheat, and muted greens with the same ink linework. Soft vignette at the parchment edges. COMPOSITION: a single unified illustration filling the frame within the parchment border, wide cinematic 16:9. Period-accurate early 1900s southern Oregon frontier town / interior — no cars, no power lines, no modern objects, no watermarks, no extra borders inside the art.
scenes: A small commercial storefront on a dirt street in Grants Pass, Oregon around 1903: a modest wooden false-front shop with a striped fabric awning stretched above the front door and a pair of display windows. Boardwalk or wooden porch under the awning, a few painted boards, a hitching post or empty dirt street in the foreground. Soft overcast light, neighboring wooden buildings blurred at the edges. Mostly monochrome charcoal and sepia ink with a soft emerald-green accent on the awning stripes or a small shop detail. Handwritten cursive caption in the lower corner: "Grants Pass, Ore. 1903". No people, or only tiny distant figures with no readable faces. Storybook ink-and-watercolor on cream parchment border.

An extreme close-up of a weathered working man's hand gripping the side rail of a wooden painter's ladder while someone climbs it — ONLY the hand on the ladder and the climber's work boot and lower shin on a rung above. NO FACES, no heads, no upper bodies. The ladder fills most of the frame; the hand is tight on the rail, knuckles strained; the boot is worn leather, mid-step. Soft side light, rain or gray sky suggested at the edge. Mostly monochrome charcoal ink with a thin emerald-green accent on a paint fleck or the ladder rope. Handwritten cursive caption: "The ladder, 1903". Storybook ink-and-watercolor on cream parchment border with green corner flourishes.

A still-life on a worn wooden office desk in Grants Pass around 1903: a thick bound book of accounts (ledger) open or closed with ruled pages and inked columns, and beside it a smaller notebook of paint formulas — dog-eared, stained with dried paint flecks of ochre and green, handwritten recipe lines barely legible. An inkwell and steel pen nearby. NO PEOPLE, NO HANDS. Warm desk lamp or window light. Mostly monochrome sepia with selective emerald-green paint stains on the formula notebook as the only vivid color. Handwritten cursive caption: "Accounts & formulas". Storybook ink-and-watercolor on cream parchment border.

A man sitting across a wooden desk facing the viewer in a modest early-1900s Grants Pass office: square jaw, dark curly hair, strong broad shoulders, wearing a dark suit jacket, white shirt, and a neat bowtie. He is solid and composed, hands resting on the desk edge or holding papers. The desk between us, a ledger or deeds faintly visible. Soft window light from one side. Full color storybook illustration with warm amber and cream tones, ink outlines, muted room greens — face fully visible, portrait-like but still illustration not photo. Handwritten cursive caption: "Samuel Frederickson". Cream parchment border with thin decorative frame.

A single wooden walking stick leaning against the edge of a worn wooden desk in a dim office — the stick is the whole subject: polished or well-used wood, curved handle, tip resting on floorboards, shaft propped on the desk lip. Soft side light, deep shadow in the room corners. NO PEOPLE. Mostly monochrome charcoal and sepia with a small emerald-green accent (a green glass inkwell or a green blotter corner on the desk). Handwritten cursive caption: "The walking stick". Storybook ink-and-watercolor on cream parchment border with green corner flourishes.
